examples: You left me too: but I won't upbraid you!
The captain upbraid his men for falling asleep.
Tim. 5:1 Do not upbraid an elderly man, but exhort him as a father, younger men as brothers.
The US Marine Corps last year had to publicly upbraid Lt Gen James Mattis after he cheerfully admitted on a panel discussion that he enjoyed war.
The Greek chorus has a privileged relationship with the protagonists: its leader often stops in mid-commentary to talk to the hero and praise or upbraid him for his intended actions.
